objectives: Understand HTML/CSS/DOM structure and how it impacts automation
Create reliable XPath and CSS selectors for common UI elements
Write basic automation scripts using a chosen language (Java/Python/Javascript)
learning_path.title: Web Automation Foundations: DOM, Locators, and Basic Scripts

objectives: Set up and run a simple UI automation project using Selenium/Cypress/Playwright
Implement basic test flows (navigation, input, assertions)
Compare framework strengths and choose an approach for a given context
learning_path.title: Getting Started with Test Automation Frameworks

objectives: Organize tests into suites and apply setup/teardown patterns
Run tests locally with Junit/Testng/Pytest/Jest
Interpret runner output and stabilize execution order and reporting basics
learning_path.title: Test Frameworks & Runners: Structuring and Executing Tests

objectives: Initialize and manage dependencies using Maven/Gradle/Npm
Standardize project structure for repeatable builds
Run tests via build tool commands and manage environment-specific configurations
learning_path.title: Build & Dependency Tools for Automation Projects

objectives: Use Git for branching, committing, and merging automation code
Apply a simple workflow for collaborating on test suites
Resolve common merge conflicts in automation projects
learning_path.title: Version Control with Git for Test Automation

objectives: Design Page Object Model structures to improve maintainability
Refactor existing tests into reusable page components
Apply naming and layering conventions for scalable automation
skills: Test Design & Automation Patterns (Page Object Model, Data-Driven)
learning_path.title: Test Design & Automation Patterns: Page Object Model

objectives: Implement data-driven tests using runner/framework capabilities
Separate test data from test logic for easier maintenance
Create reusable test utilities aligned with chosen patterns
prerequisites: Test Design & Automation Patterns (Page Object Model, Data-Driven)
skills: Test Design & Automation Patterns (Page Object Model, Data-Driven)
learning_path.title: Data-Driven Testing and Reusable Test Design

objectives: Create and validate REST/SOAP requests and responses
Automate API checks using Postman collections and Newman runs
Integrate API tests into an automation workflow alongside UI tests
learning_path.title: API Testing & Automation: Rest/Soap with Postman/Newman

objectives: Create a basic pipeline in Jenkins/Github Actions/Gitlab Ci to run tests
Configure pipeline steps for build, test execution, and artifacts
Troubleshoot common pipeline failures and flaky runs
learning_path.title: CI/CD Pipelines for Automated Testing

objectives: Install, configure, and operate open source software used in automation stacks
Evaluate compatibility and maintenance considerations for open source components
Standardize local setup steps for consistent team usage
learning_path.title: Operating Open Source Tooling in Automation Ecosystems

objectives: Align automated testing activities with production processes
Define when and how automation runs across environments
Establish repeatable execution routines to support release cadence
learning_path.title: Automation in Production Context: Process Alignment and Execution

objectives: Create a personal development plan focused on automation competencies
Identify gaps and prioritize learning across frameworks, patterns, and pipelines
Set measurable goals and review cadence for continuous improvement
learning_path.title: Professional Growth Plan for Automation Engineers

matched_occupation.description: Automation engineers research, design, and develop applications and systems for the automation of the production process. They implement technology and reduce, whenever applicable, human input to reach the full potential of industrial robotics. Automation engineers oversee the process and ensure all systems run safely and smoothly.
matched_occupation.label: automation engineer
